title: Vulca AI Presentation Launch Deck
audience: founders, product leads, design leads, and AI tool evaluators

slides.title: Vulca turns presentation taste into editable code
slides.claim: Prompt-only decks are not enough for commercial AI product launches.
slides.proof_object: A code-generated system mark plus three artifact labels: case pack, PPTX, Gemini review.

slides.title: Prompt-only decks break at launch quality
slides.claim: Fast generation often hides the design logic buyers need to trust and edit.
slides.proof_object: Three failure modes: generic story, weak hierarchy, brittle editability.

slides.title: Reference work becomes reusable design rules
slides.claim: Public launch and keynote references should shift the deck toward structured taste, not copied style.
slides.proof_object: Four source insight cards mapped to original rules, allowed use, and do-not-copy boundaries.

slides.title: The workflow is inspectable before generation
slides.claim: Vulca turns source analysis into case-pack files that guide deterministic deck code.
slides.proof_object: Pipeline diagram from sources to summaries, design memory, slide patterns, style tokens, code, and review.

slides.title: Design memory makes taste reviewable
slides.claim: The case pack stores reusable observations that humans and agents can audit.
slides.proof_object: File inventory connecting observations to editable code-generation rules.

slides.title: Generation is code-first
slides.claim: The main output is an editable PPT with native shapes, diagrams, and layout JSON.
slides.proof_object: Artifact stack: PPTX, layout JSON, contact sheet, provenance manifest, and integrity check.

slides.title: Review becomes part of the product
slides.claim: Gemini critiques the contact sheet, then a focused repair pass fixes concrete issues.
slides.proof_object: Review cycle showing scores, design issues, editability risks, accessibility risks, and repairs.

slides.title: The benchmark is baseline versus Vulca
slides.claim: Run 1 compares a prompt-only deck against the case-pack deck on ten dimensions.
slides.proof_object: Score matrix covering commercial clarity, narrative, hierarchy, coherence, editability, and rendering risk.

slides.title: Evidence decides the next primitive
slides.claim: The result should identify whether schema, layout primitives, review loops, or assets are the bottleneck.
slides.proof_object: Decision tree from score gaps and review evidence to the next product primitive.

slides.title: Make presentation design leave evidence
slides.claim: Vulca turns deck creation into a reusable, editable, reviewable workflow.
slides.proof_object: Final call to run the same workflow on the next real commercial launch deck.
